reasoning: The question requires predicting whether the 6-month T-bill rate will increase from late October 2025 to roughly January 2026. Given the value of 3.69% in October 2025, which suggests the Federal Reserve will be in or nearing the end of a rate-cutting cycle (from the 2023-2024 highs of over 5%), the general trend points toward a stabilization or continued slight decline toward the neutral rate (estimated around 2.5-3.0%). While short-term fluctuations driven by market expectations of macroeconomic data provide a non-trivial chance of an increase over any given 3-month horizon, a downward drift is macroeconomically more likely than an upward hike during this particular phase of the monetary policy cycle. Therefore, the baseline probability of an *increase* over this specific ~3-month period is estimated at roughly 35%, reflecting the slight downward drift.
